title: Worker that chains dependent jobs with transactional insert_all
prompt: Write `MyApp.Workers.DataPipelineWorker` that on successful perform/1, enqueues a fan-out of downstream jobs using a single transactional insert_all. perform/1 takes `%{"batch_id" => id}`, calls `MyApp.Pipeline.split(id)` which returns a list of `child_ids`, then enqueues one `MyApp.Workers.ChildWorker.new(%{"child_id" => cid, "batch_id" => id})` per child_id. Use `Multi.insert_all/5` (or equivalent — wrapping a list of job changesets in a Repo.transaction) so the fan-out is atomic with a status update to the batch record (`MyApp.Pipeline.mark_split(id)`). Return `:ok` on success or `{:error, reason}` on failure. Write to `lib/my_app/workers/data_pipeline_worker.ex`.
tier_rationale: Multi with Oban.insert_all for fan-out + transactional status update. Several concepts combined.
